title: Disconnect the Stacklight M12 Connector During Stacklight Removal
summary: Source-specific runbook for the documented stacklight removal action in the OptiSweep Operation and Maintenance Manual. The provided source excerpt identifies required items, references Figure 7-22 for stacklight identification, and instructs the technician to disconnect the M12 connector from the stacklight as the first removal step in stacklight replacement.
when_to_use: Use when performing the documented removal portion of stacklight replacement and the task required is to disconnect the M12 connector from the stacklight using the source-backed items and figure reference provided in Section 7.3.21 / 7.3.21.1 Removal.
not_for: Do not use as a complete stacklight replacement procedure because the packet provides only the first explicit removal step.
Do not use when safety isolation, production stop, or LOTO requirements must be confirmed from this excerpt alone.
Do not use if the stacklight assembly or M12 connector cannot be clearly identified from the provided source-backed figure and text.

responsibilities: Gather the listed PPE, tool, and replacement part identified in the source.
Use Figure 7-22 to identify the stacklight assembly and M12 connector location.
Disconnect the M12 connector from the stacklight as documented.
Stop and escalate if the connector cannot be identified or if additional undocumented removal actions are required.

safety_notes: This runbook is not support-safe for general execution from this excerpt alone because full safety isolation and complete removal details are not included in the packet.
Use the listed PPE: safety glasses and gloves.
Only the first removal instruction is present in the supplied source evidence; additional removal or replacement actions are outside this finalized source-specific runbook.

system_preconditions: Task context is stacklight replacement/removal as described in Section 7.3.21.1 Removal.
The technician can identify the stacklight assembly and M12 connector using Figure 7-22.

visual_references.description: Photo of the stacklight referenced in the Stacklight Replacement procedure and Figure 7-22 for component identification and connector location.

steps.title: Gather listed PPE, tool, and replacement part
steps.purpose: Prepare the items explicitly listed in the source before performing the documented removal action.
steps.instruction: Gather the listed items for the stacklight replacement/removal task: replacement three-segment stacklight, safety glasses, gloves, and a 7-mm wrench.
steps.expected_result: The listed PPE, tool, and replacement part are available for the task.
steps.healthy_condition: Safety glasses, gloves, 7-mm wrench, and replacement three-segment stacklight are present and ready for use.
steps.failure_condition: One or more listed items are missing or unavailable.

actions.label: Gather required items
actions.exact_action: Gather the listed items: replacement part three-segment stacklight, safety glasses, gloves, and a 7-mm wrench.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the stacklight photo as the referenced visual for the stacklight replacement procedure while preparing for the removal task.

stop_or_escalate_if: Required PPE, tool, or replacement part is not available.
The source-backed visual reference needed for identification is unavailable.

steps.title: Locate the stacklight and identify the M12 connector
steps.purpose: Ensure the correct component and connector are identified before disconnecting anything.
steps.instruction: Locate the stacklight assembly and identify the M12 connector on the stacklight using Figure 7-22 as the source-backed visual reference.
steps.expected_result: The technician has identified the stacklight assembly and the M12 connector to be disconnected.
steps.healthy_condition: The stacklight shown in Figure 7-22 matches the physical component and the M12 connector is clearly identified on the stacklight.
steps.failure_condition: The stacklight assembly or M12 connector cannot be confidently identified from the provided source-backed information.

actions.label: Identify M12 connector
actions.exact_action: Identify the M12 connector on the stacklight.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look at the stacklight assembly shown in Figure 7-22 and use it to identify the stacklight and the connector location referenced by the procedure.

stop_or_escalate_if: The M12 connector cannot be clearly identified from the source-backed information.
The physical assembly does not match the Figure 7-22 reference well enough to proceed confidently.

steps.title: Disconnect the M12 connector from the stacklight
steps.purpose: Perform the explicit removal action documented in the supplied source excerpt.
steps.instruction: Disconnect the M12 connector from the stacklight.
steps.expected_result: The M12 connector is disconnected from the stacklight.
steps.healthy_condition: The M12 connector is fully disconnected from the stacklight, completing the documented removal step provided in the source.
steps.failure_condition: The connector remains attached, cannot be disconnected, or additional undocumented removal actions appear necessary.

actions.label: Disconnect M12 connector
actions.exact_action: Disconnect the M12 connector from the stacklight.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the stacklight photo as the visual reference for the component from which the M12 connector is to be disconnected.

stop_or_escalate_if: Additional removal actions are required because this source excerpt provides only the first removal step.
The connector or component cannot be matched confidently to the Figure 7-22 reference.

success_criteria: The listed PPE, tool, and replacement part were gathered.
The stacklight assembly and M12 connector were identified using the provided source-backed figure.
The M12 connector is disconnected from the stacklight.
healthy_conditions: Required items are available before starting the removal action.
The stacklight and M12 connector can be clearly identified from Figure 7-22 and the associated source text.
The M12 connector is disconnected from the stacklight, matching the documented removal step.
failure_conditions: The stacklight assembly or M12 connector cannot be clearly identified from the provided source-backed information.
The M12 connector cannot be disconnected.
Additional removal actions are needed but are not provided in this source excerpt.
escalation_guidance: Stop if the M12 connector cannot be clearly identified from the source-backed information.
Escalate if additional removal actions are required because this source excerpt provides only the first removal step.
Escalate for SME/manual review if safety isolation, production stop, or LOTO requirements must be confirmed before continuing.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Reference image for stacklight identification and the documented M12 connector removal action.

missing_details: The supplied packet does not provide the full stacklight removal procedure beyond the first explicit removal step.
The supplied packet does not confirm whether production stop is required.
The supplied packet does not confirm whether LOTO is required for this task.
The supplied packet does not provide reinstallation or post-removal verification steps.
The supplied packet does not provide detailed connector handling technique or torque/use details for the 7-mm wrench.
